title: cynometra dauphinensis
intro: Cynometra dauphinensis, also known as the Dauphin or Doupe Tree, is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a tree native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Cynometra dauphinensis include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and burns. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion for oral consumption, and its bark and leaves can be used to create herbal preparations, including teas and ointments.
